overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and diverse social media presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, with a combined total of 3,580 followers. His content primarily focuses on academic research, publications, and professional opportunities within computational social science and disinformation studies. LinkedIn is his largest platform by audience, while Bluesky demonstrates the highest engagement rate per post. There is consistent activity across all platforms, indicating a well-managed online academic profile.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, software releases (CooRnet), and grant awards.
Posts about postdoctoral researcher roles and PhD student opportunities.
Content related to methodological discussions and upcoming conference presentations (#DisinfoConf2025).
Core topics of expertise and research focus, including coordinated inauthentic behavior and information operations.

recommendations: Leverage LinkedIn's larger audience for high-impact announcements (e.g., grants, major hiring) and consider cross-posting or linking to more detailed discussions on other platforms to drive traffic to specific communities.
Capitalize on Bluesky's high engagement rate by consistently posting research highlights, informal updates, and direct calls for engagement (e.g., Q&A sessions or prompts for replies).

Explore diversifying content on LinkedIn beyond formal announcements to include thought leadership pieces, quick summaries of research findings, or industry insights to further boost interactions from its professional audience.
Utilize a mix of content formats where possible; for instance, consider short videos or visual summaries for research announcements to enhance shareability and engagement across platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) per post, indicating a highly active and receptive audience. Content related to new publications and research opportunities performs exceptionally well here, often generating significant reposts.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has the smallest follower base and the lowest average engagement rate (4.55%). It serves as a focused hub for specific research releases and methodological discussions, catering to a niche academic community interested in detailed updates.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count (1,850) and a solid average engagement rate (4.87%). It is particularly effective for major professional announcements such as grant awards and job postings, attracting significant likes and comments from a broad professional network.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
